concerns.text: Tasks T010 and T011 (creating counter_packed.hpp and counter_padded.hpp) are listed AFTER Task T007, which already defines the creation of these exact files with the required directives. This creates a circular/redundant dependency where the consumer (T010/T011) appears to depend on the producer (T007) but actually duplicates the work. T010 and T011 should be removed or merged into T007 to ensure a single source of truth for artifact creation.

Task T018 ('Verify false sharing... by measuring throughput drop') is listed as an implementation task within US1. However, measuring throughput drop requires the multi-threaded execution logic defined in US2 (T021, T022). T018 is a consumer of US2 artifacts and cannot be executed or verified within the scope of US1. It should be moved to US2 or marked as a post-US2 validation step.

Task T005 (verify_layout.cpp) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T005 asserts struct sizes based on the definitions in T007/T010/T011. If T005 runs before T007 completes, it will fail or assert against uninitialized/missing headers. While T005 is in Phase 2, the [P] tag implies it has no dependencies on other Phase 2 tasks, which is false. It must depend on T007 completion.

Task T012 (Unit test for verify_layout.cpp) is marked 'DEPENDS_ON: T005'. However, T005 is the implementation of the utility, while T012 is a test for it. In a strict 'producer before consumer' flow, the test (T012) cannot run until the implementation (T005) is built. The dependency is correct, but the [P] tag on T013 (Integration test for build.sh) is risky if T015 (build.sh implementation) is not guaranteed to be complete. Ensure T013 is not marked [P] if it implicitly depends on T015.
